methods.description: Process for assessing the thermotolerance and longevity of olive pollen by performing viability tests at different incubation times using diaminobenzidine (DAB) reaction.

steps.description: Pollen samples were gently collected with a brush from the Petri dish.
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen samples from Petri dish

steps.description: Place pollen samples onto a 10 µL droplet of water on a microscope slide.
steps.searchable_summary: place pollen samples on water droplet on slide

steps.description: Add one droplet of 10 µL of DAB reagent on each sample.
steps.searchable_summary: add DAB reagent to pollen samples on slide

steps.description: Gently warm the microscope slides on a heating plate set at 50°C and mount with a cover slip.
steps.searchable_summary: warm slides on heating plate and mount with cover slip

steps.description: Assess pollen viability by scoring grains stained black/brown as viable and faint/colorless as not viable.
steps.searchable_summary: score pollen viability based on staining

steps.description: Measure the percentage of pollen viability at different incubation times, counting at least 100 pollen grains per slide on a total of 6 slides per cultivar per treatment.
